qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution via attacker-controlled deserialization metadata in RPC codec
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize), same file (packages/client/src/adapters/standard/rpc-json-serializer.ts:137-213), same root cause (no sanitization of __proto__ and constructor path segments in meta array during deserialization), and same package (@orpc/client).

selected_finding.title: Prototype pollution via attacker-controlled deserialization metadata in RPC codec

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable function (StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize) in the same file (packages/client/src/adapters/standard/rpc-json-serializer.ts:137-213) with the same root cause (unsanitized __proto__ path segments in the meta array). The PoC installed @orpc/client@1.13.5 (the last vulnerable version), called deserialize() with meta=[[2,'__proto__','polluted']], and confirmed Object.prototype.polluted was set to NaN. This satisfies the exploit success predicate: prototype pollution via the advisory-described RPC JSON deserializer was achieved.
evidence: PoC installed @orpc/client@1.13.5 and imported StandardRPCJsonSerializer from @orpc/client/standard
Called serializer.deserialize({}, [[2, '__proto__', 'polluted']]) which navigated __proto__ to reach Object.prototype
After exploit: ({}).polluted = NaN confirmed via Number.isNaN()
Object.prototype pollution persists for the lifetime of the process, affecting all subsequent object property lookups

poc_executions.observed_effect: After calling StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize() with meta=[[2,'__proto__','polluted']], Object.prototype.polluted was set to NaN. The PoC confirmed Number.isNaN(({}).polluted) returns true, proving prototype pollution via the RPC JSON deserializer's unsanitized __proto__ path traversal.
